cascade_form: Clustering-based sem_map cascade with strict distance threshold: AgglomerativeClustering(metric=cosine, linkage=complete, distance_threshold=0.1); pick one rep per cluster; BQ AI.GENERATE verbatim on reps; label propagation per cluster

calibration.method: AI.GENERATE_BOOL proxy with similar product-text prompt + thinking_budget=0
calibration._caveat: Q3 uses AI.GENERATE (free-form text), but token cost dominated by input + brand-name output (1-3 tokens). Calibration is a close proxy

baseline.method: sembench bigquery/q3.sql verbatim on STYLES_DETAILS — CACHED from previous run
baseline.sql:
SELECT
  id,
  AI.GENERATE(
    ('Extract the brand name from the following product description. Only return the brand name, nothing else: ',
     styles_details.productDisplayName, ' ',
     styles_details.productDescriptors.description.value),
    connection_id => 'us.connection',
    endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
  ).result AS category
FROM fashion_product_images.STYLES_DETAILS AS styles_details

cascade.method: Cascade NOT effective for Q3 (fine-grained sem_map / brand extraction). Tried: KMeans K=300 → ARI 0.86 (-0.08), cost 1.7×↓; AgglomerativeClustering τ=0.20 → ARI 0.68 (-0.26 崩); τ=0.10 → ARI 0.93 (-0.01) but only 1.18×↓ cost. All cluster-based cascades trade quality for marginal cost savings because 171 unique brands in 500 products → most clusters must be singletons to preserve ARI. Reporting cascade = baseline (= equivalent to ours BQ run)
cascade._finding_note: Paper Table 4 reports DASE+NN as X (not applicable) for Q3 because 'embedding-based prefiltering is less accurate for semantic merge (M) tasks' (paper §5.1). Our cascade experiments confirm: rich output spaces (brand-name extraction) are inherently hard for embedding-based pre-filtering

score._note: ours_cascade = ours_bq because cascade doesn't help on Q3 sem_map
